title: Aneesh Chaganty on Leaving Google to Make 'Searching'
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Aneesh Chaganty the week 'Searching' opens wide: quitting his Google job for a movie told entirely on computer screens, writing a script that isn't a script, losing and re-landing John Cho, and shooting the whole thing in 13 days.

films.title: Searching
films.note: His first feature, a thriller that plays out entirely on computer screens: John Cho's David Kim hunts for his missing teenage daughter through her laptop. Grew out of a Basilevs pitch to follow 'Unfriended' with a screen-based anthology; Chaganty and co-writer Sev Ohanian broke it open with a seven-minute opening montage he pitched as 'Up meets a Google commercial.' Shot in 13 days off a scriptment rather than a screenplay, then cut over a year and a half. Premiered at Sundance; Sony bought it 12 hours later.

films.title: Unfriended
films.note: The earlier Basilevs/Timur Bekmambetov screen-based movie that seeded the pitch and became the comp Chaganty kept trying to escape; John Cho watched it before initially passing.

films.title: Up
films.note: The reference point for the wordless prologue told through 17 years of a family's home computer; he pitched the sequence as 'Up meets a Google commercial.'

films.title: The Oath
films.note: Sean caught Cho in a supporting role the day after screening 'Searching' and wanted more of him; opens that October.

films.title: Tigertail
films.note: Named as a just-announced Cho project with Alan Yang, co-creator of 'Master of None,' as evidence Cho should be getting leads.

films.title: Run
films.note: Chaganty's next feature, described here as an in-the-works contained thriller about a mother and daughter, shot with regular cameras in regular spaces.

films.title: Election
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he'd seen: an old favorite he rewatched for Alexander Payne and Jim Taylor's framing, which he says he plans to steal for a thriller.

Ads: Drive Sober or Get Pulled Over (NHTSA)
